steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The subject of the blank is "MindMaze's technology" — a singular noun phrase. The phrase "alongside other such neuromodulation technologies that function by stimulating nervous system structures" is a parenthetical modifier, not part of the subject. The blank is the main verb of that clause.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices, confirm the right one
steps.content:
The verb must agree with the singular subject "MindMaze's technology" and form a complete predicate:
  • Choice A — 'paving': a participle, not a finite verb — leaves the clause without a complete verb, creating a fragment. ✗
  • Choice B — 'is paving': singular present progressive — agrees with the singular subject and forms a complete, grammatical predicate. ✓
  • Choice C — 'have paved': plural present perfect — disagrees with the singular subject "MindMaze's technology." ✗
  • Choice D — 'are paving': plural present progressive — also disagrees with the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
